overview: This Pauper B/R/G Spy Combo deck leverages Land Grant and Generous Ent to rapidly accelerate mana and deploy Masked Vandal alongside other threats. By playing multiple turns ahead, you aim to overwhelm opponents with efficient creature deployment and targeted board control. Success hinges on protecting your ramp pieces while sequencing spells to maximize tempo and card advantage.
win_conditions: Deploying multiple Masked Vandals to remove opponent threats while dealing direct damage
Using Generous Ent and Land Grant to accelerate into unblockable or large creatures
Establishing early board presence and outpacing control or midrange strategies

keep_criteria: Hands with at least two land acceleration spells like Land Grant or Generous Ent
Hands containing Masked Vandal alongside early land drops
Hands with a mix of mana sources and one threat
mulligan_criteria: Hands with zero land acceleration or threats
Hands with three or more lands but no creatures
Hands lacking early game interaction or ramp

early_game: Play lands and cast Generous Ent or Land Grant to accelerate mana
Deploy early creatures to apply pressure and protect ramp pieces
Prioritize drawing into Masked Vandal for board control
mid_game: Use accumulated mana to play multiple Masked Vandals and other threats
Counter opponent key plays by targeting them with Vandal counters
Search for Forests and basic lands to ensure consistent casting of combo pieces
late_game: Deploy large threats accelerated by ramp spells to finish the game
Use Generous Ent card draw to find answers to late game threats
Maintain board presence while outdrawing and outmaneuvering opponents

key_cards.name: Land Grant
key_cards.notes: Cast early to fetch a basic land, enabling faster casting of Generous Ent and Masked Vandal. Use it to fix mana or accelerate into turn three threats.

key_cards.name: Masked Vandal
key_cards.notes: Deploy to apply pressure and remove opponent key creatures with minus one minus one counters. Prioritize targeting high impact threats to clear the path for your combo.

key_cards.name: Generous Ent
key_cards.notes: Play to search for a Forest, ensuring consistent green mana for Land Grant and other spells. Use the card draw effect to find additional threats or answers.

sequencing_tips: Cast Land Grant before Generous Ent to ensure you have the green mana needed for the Ent ability
Hold up black mana for Masked Vandal casting cost while using green mana for ramp spells
Prioritize playing Generous Ent on turns when you can immediately use the drawn Forest for a follow up spell
Save Masked Vandal triggered ability for opponent most threatening creature rather than using it preemptively

matchups.advice: Prioritize playing Masked Vandal early to remove aggressive creatures and stabilize the board. Use Generous Ent to accelerate into larger blockers.
Play around removal by holding up counters or playing threats on your opponent turn if possible. Use Land Grant to ensure you have lands to play around counterspells.
Race them by deploying multiple Masked Vandals quickly. Use Generous Ent to outdraw them and find answers to their value creatures.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Land Grant and Generous Ent provide acceleration, while Masked Vandal serves as the primary threat, aligning with the Spy Combo archetype in Pauper.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Signature cards emphasize land acceleration and creature deployment, typical of combo strategies in Pauper.

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Mana base optimization and trigger timing are standard piloting principles for ramp combo decks in Pauper.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Standard Pauper meta threats that interact with ramp combo strategies.
